evidence_quotes.quote: In order to become more precise, an online multiplayer should not be kept as a single player.
However, if the multiplayer is the main focus of a game, it should be possible to use the Multiplayer ceasing to use it at the end of the support.
I would like to request that companies be prohibited from discontinuing the service after purchasing a product, making it completely unusable.
Companies should also be prohibited from using a product, or forcing them to remove a product from a private PC by a third-party supplier.

paragraphs.text: I would like to request that companies be prohibited from discontinuing the service after purchasing a product, making it completely unusable. I do not wish to say that I want the company to continue a game, software or server infinitely, but to keep the product usable for the buyer. I would also like to point out that the product remains usable, not all features remain available. In order to become more precise, an online multiplayer should not be kept as a single player. However, if the multiplayer is the main focus of a game, it should be possible to use the Multiplayer ceasing to use it at the end of the support. There are many opportunities, such as a firm, to achieve this, many of them could cost little, especially if this is planned from the outset. Companies should also be prohibited from using a product, or forcing them to remove a product from a private PC by a third-party supplier. This has only been done for a short time by Ubisoft at the game ‘The Crew’. I thank you for your attentiveness and time to read my feedback and apologise for mistakes in law.
